stories.title: Orbán’s media slop spread poison beyond Hungary. Luckily, fearless, fact-based reporting endures | Beata Balogová
stories.summary: Across Europe, public service journalism is a shield for democracy. But we have to decide if we want to fight for its survival For 16 years, Viktor Orbán’s government poured millions of euros of public money into thinktanks, institutions and media outlets sympathetic to its illiberal views – not only in Hungary but beyond its borders . In Slovakia, for instance , where a sizeable Hungarian minority lives, Budapest is alleged to have sent millions of euros to favoured media organisations. Many in

stories.title: ‘Strong support’ for excluding fighting-age men from EU’s Ukraine refugee scheme
stories.summary: LUXEMBOURG — There is “strong support” among EU home affairs ministers to exclude men who could be called up to the military from Europe’s temporary protection scheme for Ukrainian refugees, said Swedish Migration Minister Johan Forssell. The temporary status scheme — under which more than 4 million people who fled Ukraine have been given the right to work, live and study in European countries — will expire in March 2027. The directive has already been extended several times an

stories.title: Friday briefing: How Gaza, Lebanon and Iran have found themselves caught in an escalation without end
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: ​Global powers​ are focused on oil markets and elections​ but those living through conflict in the Middle East feel abandoned Good morning. It’s been another week of brinkmanship via Truth Social and ceasefires broken before they’ve been announced. While US president Donald Trump claims an agreement with Iran could happen soon, for those living in the Middle East it does not feel like peace is anywhere near. People have seen more bombs dropped in Lebanon this week; and the

stories.title: Ukraine: Marine drone explodes in Romanian port
stories.summary: A naval drone exploded in the Romanian port of Constanta on Friday morning, causing no injuries. Ukraine's navy claimed ownership of the device which it said had been "jammed" by Russian electronic warfare. DW has more.

stories.title: Moment sea drone explodes in Romania's Constanta port
stories.summary: Video shows the moment a sea drone exploded in Romania's Black Sea port of Constanta. Authorities said the drone self-destructed after the area was secured and evacuated, and no casualties were reported.

stories.title: Romania: TV reporter flees live on air after drone explosion in Constanța
stories.summary: A live television report from Romania's Black Sea port of Constanța was abruptly interrupted on 5 June after a maritime drone exploded nearby, heightening concerns over the regional impact of the war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Ukraine strikes cargo ships and admits Romania drone blast as Putin prepares for key speech
stories.summary: Ukraine says it struck five ships carrying illegal cargo in the Sea of Azov and in coastal waters of Russian-occupied territories.

stories.title: Trump says Zelenskyy-Putin meeting would be 'great' after Ukraine president's letter - video
stories.summary: Donald Trump says that to end the Ukraine war both sides would have to make compromises, which he took credit for suggesting without explaining further. The letter sets out Zelenskyy’s view of the four-year-old conflict and says that while Ukrainians’ resilience remains

stories.summary: Ukrainian president proposes meeting in neutral third country as Trump says both sides have to ‘make compromises’ ‘You can stop your war’: Zelenskyy’s open letter to Putin – in full The Ukrainian president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has called for face-to-face negotiations in a public letter addressed directly to the Russian president, Vladimir Putin. stories.title: Nem véletlen a gesztus, Magyar Péternek még szüksége lehet a Mi Hazánkra
stories.summary: A hétfői parlamenti ülésen két meglepő jelenet is volt: a miniszterelnök felajánlotta az egyik vizsgálóbizottság vezetését Toroczkai Lászlónak, a Mi Hazánk két képviselője pedig ellenzéktől szokatlan módon elfogadta a tiszás miniszterek válaszát. Közeledés vagy csak pillanatnyi politikai érdek? Megnéztük, hogy alakul a két párt dinamikája a parlamentben.

stories.summary: Already submitted applications will be considered pending and will be processed, but expedited procedures for work permits will no longer be an option for those from the Philippines, Georgia, and Armenia.

stories.title: The EU should fast-track Ukraine’s membership of the club – it has the most to gain | Mujtaba Rahman
stories.summary: Many EU governments oppose it, but the strategic advantages of swift admission could be immense Russia’s war on Ukraine is now in its fifth year and a ceasefire remains elusive. The US’s attention is divided, limiting external pressure for compromise, while Moscow and Kyiv both still believe they can strengthen their respective negotiating positions through battlefield gains. At some point, however, a deal will have to be done. The parameters of that deal are already understood by negotiators on

stories.title: What the Hellenic! Why is Christopher Nolan’s new Greek epic entirely devoid of Greeks?
stories.summary: Set to be this year’s biggest blockbuster, The Odyssey’s cast has been selected to ‘represent the world’. Fair enough – except that one key country seems to have gone completely unrepresented … There are the American accents, gleaming body suits and a muddy Dunkirk palette. And then there is Lupita Nyong’o as Helen of Troy, a casting choice that recently drew racist attacks from the usual moaners of the internet , including Elon Musk, who complained it wasn’t authentic. stories.title: Trump never wanted to invade Greenland, US envoy to EU says
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump’s threats to invade Greenland earlier this year were misunderstood, the American ambassador to the European Union said Friday. “It got interpreted that we were somehow threatening Greenland’s territorial integrity,” but “the president never said we were going to invade,” Andrew Puzder told attendees at the Brussels Economic Security Forum. During his second term in office, Trump has repeatedly floated the idea of annexing Greenland,

stories.title: Pro-Palestinian activists force EU commissioner to move meeting with arms manufacturer
stories.summary: Belgian local authorities asked the European Commission to move a meeting between defense chief Andrius Kubilius and a French arms manufacturer on Friday due to security concerns. Kubilius had been scheduled to visit a Thales manufacturing site in Herstal, near Liège, together with Walloon Minister-President Adrien Dolimont. But as some pro-Palestinian activists gathered outside the factory, Belgian police claimed it could no longer guarantee the commissioner’s safety, according to local media r

stories.title: US Senate passes $70 billion funding for ICE, Border Patrol
stories.summary: Senators voted 52-47 to approve funds set to finance the immigration forces for another three years, through the rest of Donald Trump's term. The bill still needs to clear the House; that vote is expected next week.

stories.title: Ukraine aid package passes U.S. House, bucking Republican leaders and Trump
stories.summary: A new tranche of military assistance for Ukraine and sanctions on Russia passed the House on Thursday, delivering a fresh bipartisan blow to President Donald Trump’s foreign policy from Capitol Hill. The measure passed despite Republican leadership’s resistance, with 18 GOP lawmakers supporting Democrats in a 226-195 vote. It’s a strong show of support for Kyiv and the first time a standalone aid package has passed either chamber during Trump’s second term. Some Republicans have resisted allocat

stories.title: EU watchdog says von der Leyen should have kept Macron’s Mercosur text message
stories.summary: PARIS — The EU’s transparency watchdog has accused the Commission of maladministration after failing to keep a text message from French President Emmanuel Macron to Ursula von der Leyen, the head of the EU executive, urging a freeze in talks on a major trade deal with Latin America. Macron’s text message in early 2024,  exclusively reported by POLITICO , sought to halt talks on the trade accord with the Mercosur bloc within sight of the finish line. The deal was only approved at the beginni

stories.title: Macron invites club of Ukraine allies to attend Bastille Day
stories.summary: PARIS — French President Emmanuel Macron on Friday invited Ukraine’s allies to attend France’s Bastille Day celebrations in Paris on July 14. Speaking on the margins of the EU-Western Balkans summit in Montenegro, Macron said the so-called Coalition of the Willing nations would discuss support for Ukraine and attend France’s annual military parade. stories.title: Jamaican police officer charged with murder after woman shot during protest
stories.summary: Andrew Wilson appears in court over killing of Latoya Bulgin at protest over a police shooting days earlier Authorities in Jamaica have taken the rare step of charging a police officer with murder after he was accused of shooting a 45-year-old woman in a case that prompted violent protests. According to the Independent Commission of Investigations (Indecom), Constable Andrew Wilson appeared in court on Wednesday and was denied bail. Another hearing is scheduled for mid-June. stories.title: UK shoppers return to high street as warm weather brings respite from shadow of war
stories.summary: British Retail Consortium figures show footfall rose in May, with consumer confidence improving after spending squeeze Greater numbers of consumers went shopping last month as spring sunshine brought welcome relief to retailers, which have faced a squeeze on spending since the US-Israel war on Iran. stories.title: William Hill owner agrees £243m takeover by Greek casino and lottery firm
stories.summary: Evoke had been in talks for two months with Bally’s Intralot, which has extensive international operations The owner of William Hill and the 888 online casino brand has agreed a £243m takeover by the Greek casino and lottery operator Bally’s Intralot. stories.title: Unseen Edith Wharton short story is published more than a century later

stories.title: UK-EU ‘reset’ summit may still happen next month despite delay speculation

stories.title: Man jailed over 2003 Salford rape for which Andrew Malkinson was wrongly imprisoned
stories.summary: Paul Quinn, 52, to serve 24 years for attack that led to one of worst miscarriages of justice in modern British history A man who evaded justice for nearly two decades has been jailed for 21 years for a “savage” rape for which Andrew Malkinson was wrongly imprisoned. stories.summary: As criticism of Instagram, TikTok and X grows in the EU, developers have been working on European social media apps. But can they compete? DW answers the key questions.

stories.title: SSRI antidepressants are often 'carelessly prescribed'
stories.summary: SSRIs, like Prozac and Zoloft, are among the world's most-prescribed psychiatric drugs. These antidepressants help a lot of people. But they don't always work. In fact, some experts suggest they are overprescribed.

stories.title: India news: Putin hails Delhi ties, Trump calls Modi 'good friend'
stories.summary: Russian leader Vladimir Putin said that India’s cooperation with the US won't harm India-Russia relations, while US President Donald Trump said he has a "good relationship" with Prime Minister Narendra Modi. DW has more.

stories.title: Australia seizes 100,000 illegal cockroaches in record bug bust
stories.summary: The haul of live insects included dubia cockroaches and Madagascar hissing cockroaches. Authorities said it was the country's biggest-ever seizure of illegal exotic invertebrates.

stories.title: EU to advance expansion plans at Balkans summit
stories.summary: European leaders are gathering in Montenegro with EU expansion high on the agenda. Montenegro is a leading candidate for accession while, further east, Ukraine and Moldova also harbor ambitions.

stories.title: Environment wins of the week
stories.summary: Good news about the state of the planet might seem to be in short supply, but it's out there. Each week, DW's environment desk brings you what went right.

stories.title: Slovenia's political survivor Janez Jansa is back
stories.summary: The new coalition government of Slovenia's veteran leader Janez Jansa is already advancing divisive policies that some warn could deepen polarization.

stories.title: Middle East: UN doubles Lebanon aid appeal amid Israel war
stories.summary: The UN says it is seeking an additional $331.5 million to tackle a worsening humanitarian crisis in Lebanon. Meanwhile, Israel is carrying out fresh strikes on the south of the country. Follow DW for the latest.

stories.title: Germany news: National team won't win World Cup, fans think
stories.summary: According to a poll, most Germans do not believe that Julian Nagelsmann's team will become world champions. Meanwhile, the German foreign minister is visiting Mexico. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Germany's Iran war lessons after 100 days
stories.summary: The war waged by the US and Israel against Iran has now lasted 100 days. In that time, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has drastically changed his stance on the war.

stories.title: Iran at war: Stability masks deepening crises
stories.summary: One hundred days after the Iran war began, the Islamic Republic appears stable. But beneath the surface, economic strain, social unrest and growing repression point to deeper instability.

stories.title: Why businesses are offering 'quiet hours'
stories.summary: More peace and quiet, less stress — the Quiet Hour is an initiative for people with invisible disabilities. An increasing number of stores and institutions in Germany are launching similar campaigns.

stories.title: North Korea quietly ramps up its nuclear program
stories.summary: With Washington's attention elsewhere, North Korea has slowly been building up its nuclear weapons program. This week, Kim Jong Un took a publicity tour of a new nuclear fuel facility while promising to build more bombs.

stories.title: German asylum benefits cuts violate EU law, top court rules
stories.summary: In a victory for rejected asylum-seekers, the European Court of Justice has ruled they must be given more than just the bare minimum to survive. But as the EU's new migration pact kicks in, will relief be short-lived?

stories.title: Xi Jinping set for North Korea trip on Monday in first visit to Pyongyang in seven years
stories.summary: The trip comes just weeks after Xi separately hosted US President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing.

stories.title: US sanctions Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel as Trump adds pressure to island’s leadership
stories.summary: Thursday’s penalties, which follow Trump signing of an executive order expanding sanctions against the island, freeze individuals’ property and bank accounts in the US.

stories.title: China cracks down on soft porn, violence and materialism in viral micro dramas
stories.summary: Micro dramas have surged in popularity, but drawn criticism for often sensationalist content.

stories.title: Xi Jinping to meet Kim Jong Un in rare visit to North Korea
stories.summary: It is taking place weeks after Xi met the leaders of the US and Russia - two countries that loom large over North Korea's foreign policy.

stories.title: Ex-wife of Dubai ruler's nephew in custody, prosecutors say
stories.summary: Zeynab Javadli had not been in contact with any of her friends since Tuesday.

stories.title: Australian authorities seize more than 100,000 live exotic cockroaches in huge bug bust
stories.summary: The seizure included dubia cockroaches and Madagascar hissing cockroaches, both of which cannot be legally imported into Australia or kept, bred, or sold.

stories.title: Denmark probes death of humpback whale that captivated Germany
stories.summary: Denmark has launched a post-mortem examination on Timmy, a humpback whale whose struggle to return to the sea captured public attention in Germany and beyond. Scientists hope to determine why the animal died weeks after being returned to open waters.

stories.title: Thousands join Jerusalem pride parade under tight security
stories.summary: Thousands joined Jerusalem Pride parade on 4 June, under heavy security, marching for equality and acceptance in a deeply conservative, religiously significant city.

stories.title: Venezuela's Dancing Devils revive centuries-old Corpus Christi rite
stories.summary: Children making their First Communion were accompanied by masked "Dancing Devils" in Tarmas, Venezuela. The centuries-old Corpus Christi celebration symbolises the triumph of good over evil and remains a key cultural tradition.

stories.title: 'Extremely intelligent' bear on the run in Japan after injuring four people
stories.summary: Authorities said that a bear in north-east Japan has eluded capture by opening a window.

stories.title: US actor James Handy stabbed to death, with girlfriend's son arrested
stories.summary: Handy, 81, is known for his roles in films including Jumanji and Top Gun: Maverick.

stories.title: Smart pipelines: Can AI protect the world’s energy lifelines?
stories.summary: As ageing pipelines face growing risks, the energy industry is increasingly turning to AI and smart monitoring systems to improve their safety and efficiency.

stories.title: US journalist pleads guilty to working as China's agent
stories.summary: Thomas Weir Pauken II, 50, knowingly worked with people employed by the Chinese government for years.

stories.title: Fury in France after child murder suspect's criminal record released
stories.summary: A man suspected of murdering an 11-year-old girl had previously been identified as a potential child molester, records show.

stories.title: Five sailors killed as cargo ships struck in Sea of Azov
stories.summary: The cargo vessels Natra and Zirkon — both bearing hallmarks of Russia's shadow fleet and heading to load grain at a port linked to looted Ukrainian supplies — were struck overnight near Taganrog.

stories.title: Lebanon: UN clears debris in Dibbine after Israeli withdrawal
stories.summary: UN peacekeepers and heavy machinery entered the southern Lebanese village of Dibbine on 5 June to clear debris left by recent fighting.

stories.title: Germany: Bavarian lake procession marks centuries-old boat tradition for Corpus Christi
stories.summary: Hundreds of worshippers gathered in Bavaria on 4 June for Seehausen's traditional Corpus Christi lake procession. The event, held annually since 1935, combines religious devotion with local heritage on the waters of Staffelsee.

stories.title: Nearly 50 people die of thirst in Sahara desert after lorry breaks down
stories.summary: Only two survived after trekking more than 50km (30 miles) across the desert to alert the authorities.

stories.title: Singer Fally Ipupa's pride at being given major DR Congo honour
stories.summary: He follows the late great Papa Wemba in becoming a knight of the National Order of the Leopard.

stories.title: Truck breakdown in Niger strands passengers and leaves 49 dead in Sahara Desert
stories.summary: The 49 victims were buried in mass graves at the scene in what officials called a “particularly delicate and emotionally exhausting task” for the survivors.

stories.title: Hospitality jobs boom as US prepares for World Cup
stories.summary: It is the third month in a row US jobs figures have beaten expectations.

stories.title: EU backs Pashinyan on the eve of Armenia's defining elections
stories.summary: The EU endorsed Armenia’s PM Nikol Pashinyan and announced a support package to counter Russian sanctions, just days before Armenia’s historic elections. Meanwhile, Pashinyan said he would go to Moscow after the vote and proclaimed that "abandoning Karabakh was my greatest service to Armenia".

stories.title: Dear Microsoft, enough is enough
stories.summary: Dear Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella, As browser developers, we believe PC users’ choices should be free and respected, not controlled, distorted or ignored. That principle unites the Browser Choice Alliance in advancing a digital world where PC users control their own online experience, can choose their preferred browser without manipulation or interference, trust their preferences will be honored and participate in a marketplace where browsers compete on merit. The approach Microsoft has taken is

stories.title: The Brexit Referendum: Ten years on
stories.summary: Ten years — and six prime ministers — ago Britain was on the brink of voting to leave the European Union. Host Patrick Baker was in his first job as a TV news producer during the referendum campaign. A decade on, he’s gone back to the people who lived through its biggest moments to find out what was really happening behind the scenes, and how those events changed British politics forever. Matthew Elliott, chief executive of Vote Leave, recounts the brutal battle with rival group Leave.EU to beco

stories.title: Dead but deportable: US immigration judge signed order to eject teen murder victim
stories.summary: North Carolina judge said Levi Mendez-Maldonado failed to show up in court – even after being told he had died in 2024 An immigration judge in Charlotte, North Carolina , recently ordered the deportation of a young man who was killed in 2024, citing his failure to appear in court. Judge Amy Lee ordered the removal of Levi Mendez-Maldonado in absentia on 21 May. Mendez-Maldonado, originally from Honduras, came to the United States as an unaccompanied minor at age 17 and was murdered in a shooting

stories.title: Protesters clash with police in Albania over Jared Kushner-backed luxury resort – video
stories.summary: Protests in Albania over a proposed luxury resort backed by Jared Kushner , Donald Trump’s son-in-law, are expected to intensify after opponents rejected an offer from the country’s prime minister 'to discuss solutions'. Footage showed police firing water cannon at protesters as thousands of people took to the streets of Tirana for a third day on Wednesday, some of them holding inflatable flamingos to symbolise the environmental damage they fear the project could cause, amid growing calls for it

stories.title: Civilians flee as Somali troops and opposition-allied militias trade fire in Mogadishu
stories.summary: Violence flares before protests on Thursday over president’s decision to remain in office after his term expired Fierce clashes have taken place between government troops and militias allied with the opposition in Somalia’s capital, Mogadishu, damaging property and forcing some civilians to flee. In the runup to the fighting, which started on Wednesday afternoon, opposition leaders embedded with militias set up positions in their clan strongholds the city. Continue reading...

stories.title: Watch: New footage shows moment Iranian drone struck Kuwait airport
stories.summary: One person was killed and more than 60 injured in the Iranian drone strike on Wednesday.

stories.title: Missing Sherpa guide found alive on Everest after funeral rites had begun
stories.summary: Climbing support team rescue Hillary Dawa Sherpa almost a week on from when he was last seen A Nepali guide who was believed to have died on Mount Everest has been found crawling to base camp a week after going missing – and after his funeral rites had begun. Dawa Sherpa, also known as Hillary Dawa Sherpa after the famous climber Edmund Hillary, was last seen on 29 May but did not reach base camp with other climbing groups. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hezbollah rejects Israel-Lebanon truce as Trump scrambles to end Iran war
stories.summary: Group calls ceasefire a ‘roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people’, throwing regional peace talks into doubt Hezbollah has rejected a US-brokered ceasefire plan agreed by the Lebanese and Israeli governments, throwing the future of a truce in Lebanon and regional peace negotiations into question. The group’s leader, Naim Qassem, called the plan a “roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people” in a statement on Thursday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canada endorses embattled marine park’s plan to relocate 30 beluga whales
stories.summary: Beluga whales, which Marineland threatened to euthanize in 2025, will be moved to sanctuaries in Spain or across US Canada and an embattled marine park have reached a tentative deal on the future of 30 beluga whales , ending a saga that has captivated the public and angered animal rights groups. The federal fisheries ministry announced this week that all of Marineland’s belugas would be shipped to either Spain or one of four locations in the US , ending whale captivity in Canada. Continue readin

stories.title: US imposes new sanctions on Cuban president and Castro family members
stories.summary: US secretary of state Marco Rubio says anyone providing services to listed entities ‘is at risk of sanctions themselves’ The United States has announced fresh economic sanctions on Cuba’s president and some of his immediate family, alongside members of the Castro family, in Washington’s latest ramping up of pressure on its communist-led neighbour. Among those targeted were the son and a grandson of former president Raúl Castro, who no longer holds an official position but remains a key figure on

stories.title: Why Yad Vashem is coming to Germany
stories.summary: "Remember" — every child in Israel knows the meaning of this Jewish commandment: Never forget the Holocaust. Now Yad Vashem wants to make sure people in Germany remember it too.

stories.title: EU seeking ways to speed up Western Balkans membership
stories.summary: European Council President Antonio Costa said new ways need to be found to speed up Western Balkans membership in the EU. His comments come ahead of an upcoming summit in Montenegro.

stories.title: Trump critic John Bolton to plead guilty in documents case
stories.summary: Donald Trump's former national security adviser-turned critic is accused of mishandling classified ⁠documents while he wrote an unflattering memoir about Trump's first term.

stories.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy proposes Putin meeting on ending war
stories.summary: The leaders of Russia and Ukraine have both signaled confidence, and willingness to talk. Putin shows no sign of backing off from his demands, while Zelenskyy said Ukraine would keep fighting for "survival."

stories.title: Dutch police arrest 4 men over drugging, rape of partners
stories.summary: The men allegedly filmed the sexual assaults and shared the footage. The case is reminiscent of that of French woman Gisele Pelicot.

stories.title: UNIFIL peacekeeper killed and two others injured in shelling in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: The UN peacekeeping mission called attacks on peacekeepers 'grave violations of international humanitarian law' and has opened an investigation into the incident. It is unclear where the shelling originated from.

stories.title: Man charged with bringing bomb in his carry-on bag to a California airport
stories.summary: Before the device was removed from the airport, officers put a bomb blast suppression blanket over it and taped off the immediate area, according to the federal complaint.

stories.title: Marilyn Monroe auction features star's make-up and gowns on 100th birthday
stories.summary: The auction has already brought in thousands in bids and includes intimate items from her life.

stories.title: Paris and Berlin push 'gradual integration' plan into EU ahead of Balkans summit
stories.summary: Merz and Macron pitch "building blocks" approach to EU enlargement to revitalise accession talks, ahead of this week's Western Balkans summit.

stories.title: Gaza: Families mourn victims after overnight Israeli strikes that killed at least nine
stories.summary: Families gathered in Gaza City to mourn relatives killed on 4 June after overnight Israeli airstrikes in Gaza City, according to local hospitals.

stories.title: Gaza city residents survey destruction after overnight Israeli airstrike
stories.summary: Gaza residents surveyed extensive damage on 4 June after an overnight Israeli airstrike hit an apartment in the Sheikh Radwan neighbourhood of Gaza City.

stories.title: Watch: Guide stranded on Everest for six days rescued
stories.summary: The BBC's Rajini Vaidyanathan pieces together how events unfolded.

stories.title: 'It looks black' - Americans react to Reflecting Pool's completed paint job
stories.summary: After weeks of renovation, water has begun refilling the historic pool, which has been coated in a colour named "American flag blue".

stories.title: Here's why counting votes in California is taking so long
stories.summary: It could take weeks - or even a month - before we know who won elections in California and Los Angeles.

stories.title: Tough cookies: How pop group Le Sserafim overcame band tensions and internet trolls
stories.summary: The K-pop band say accepting their flaws and embracing humour took them to a new level of success.

stories.title: Mangrove forests are healing after decades of human destruction
stories.summary: Swampy mangrove forests are staging a surprise comeback - which is good news for coastal communities and the climate.

stories.title: How Trump's White House ballroom plan has doubled in size and cost over a year
stories.summary: BBC Verify examines how the biggest change to the White House in decades has transformed in the last year.

stories.title: US plans to fight flesh-eating screwworm outbreak with flies and dogs
stories.summary: The insect, which has been found in America for the first time since 1966, is a growing concern in the southern US.

stories.title: Hezbollah rejects renewed ceasefire agreed by Israel and Lebanon
stories.summary: The United States announced the ceasefire agreement on Wednesday night following a fresh round of talks.

stories.title: A FIFA kitiltotta a műanyag palackokat a világbajnokság stadionjaiból
stories.summary: Pedig pusztulat hőséget várnak bizonyos helyszínekre.

stories.title: Németországot nem választották be az ENSZ Biztonsági Tanácsába, politikai össztűz zúdult Friedrich Merzre
stories.summary: Ausztria és Portugália is beelőzte őket, a német külügy az orosz ellenlobbit sejti a háttérben.

stories.title: Lefoglalt egy nagy BMW-t a NAV, majd átpasszolta a rendőrségnek
stories.summary: A törvény megengedi, hogy a bűncselekmény miatt lefoglalt autókat átadják a rendvédelmi szerveknek.

stories.summary: A kutatók szerint ez az első alkalom, hogy egy oltás legfontosabb összetevőjét teljes egészében mesterséges intelligencia tervezte meg, majd embereken is kipróbálták azt.

stories.summary: A királyi rezidenciákról készült számvevőszéki jelentés szerint András, a családja és a személyzete összesen 12 ingatlannal rendelkezett.

stories.summary: Az első becslés alapján a KSH annyit közölt, hogy a feldolgozóipari alágak többségében csökkent a termelés volumene az előző év azonos hónapjához képest.

stories.summary: A férfi versenyképességi támogatásra pályázott, de a vád szerint olyan adatokat adott meg a külügyminisztériumnak, amelyek nem feleltek meg a valóságnak.

stories.summary: Jövő héten benyújtják az uniós pénzek felhasználásához szükséges korrupcióellenes és átláthatósági törvénycsomagot. Nem jöhet külföldi munkaerő a Fülöp-szigetekről, Georgiából és Örményországból. Vizsgálják a Honvédelmi Minisztérium korábbi vezetésének ügyeit. stories.title: Táplálkozhat-e a szerelmi vágy a gyászból?
stories.summary: Az 1954-ben, mindössze 31 évesen meghalt svéd Stig Dagerman könyvében a lélekelemzés olyan mélyre megy, ahová talán be sem lenne szabad pillantanunk. Kritika.

stories.summary: Eredetileg június elsején élesedett volna a tiltás.

stories.title: A kormány felülvizsgálja a hulladékgazdálkodási rendszert, és szigorítaná az akkumulátoripar szabályozását
stories.summary: Megszüntetik azt a szabályt is, ami alapján miniszteri döntéssel lehetett kijelölni akkumulátor-újrafeldolgozó üzemek helyszíneit.

stories.title: Égésgyorsítóval leöntötte, majd felgyújtotta egy rendőr autóját egy férfi a gödöllői rendőrkapitányság előtt
stories.summary: A többszörösen büntetett előéletű férfi indítéka egyelőre nem ismert.

stories.summary: Az Otthon Start Program kedvezményeit látva alaposan belenyúltak a mézesbödönbe a lakáshitel-felvevők. A program hatására az adósok nominálisan és éves jövedelmük arányában is sokkal magasabb hitelösszegre szerződtek, mint ami korábban megszokott volt.

stories.title: Akár két év börtönbüntetést is kaphatna az a politikus, aki lényegi információt hagy ki a vagyonnyilatkozatából
stories.summary: Korrupcióellenes törvénycsomagot visz a kormány az országgyűlés elé. Az Integritás Hatóság mozgástere jelentősen bővül, gyanús közbeszerzéseknél és rendőrségi eljárásoknál is közbeléphet majd.

stories.title: Az Egyesült Államok újabb szankciókat léptet életbe a kubai vezetés ellen
stories.summary: A szankciók az elnököt és a Castro családot érintik.

stories.title: Megjelent, mennyi támogatást kapnak a parlamentbe jutott pártok
stories.summary: A pártalapítványaikkal együtt a Tiszának és a Fidesznek is milliárdos összeg fölött jutott.

stories.title: Elfogadták az új szerződést, egyelőre nem lesz újabb színészsztrájk Hollywoodban

stories.title: Gyömrő adócsalással gyanúsított volt polgármestere is indulna az időközi választáson
stories.summary: Gyenes Leventének ehhez 18 ajánlást kell összeszednie.

stories.summary: Már idén nyáron is előfordulhat, hogy a vízmélység egyes területeken 30 centiméter alá csökken.

stories.summary: A 88 éves rendezőt a Ralph Fiennesszal és Viggo Mortensennel készülő, A gyertyák csonkig égnek című film apropóján kérdezték.

stories.summary: Mindössze 1 perc 13 másodperc alatt törték be az üzlet üvegajtaját, majd felmarkoltak több tízezer dollár értékű Pokémon-kártyát és távoztak.

stories.title: Egy videó szerint jóval súlyosabb volt az amerikai repülőgép-hordozón márciusban kitört tűz, mint azt a haditengerészet bevallotta
stories.summary: A tengerészek szerint a hajó tűzoltó rendszere nem működött, így a legénység 30 órán át küzdött a lángokkal.

stories.summary: A férfi sokadjára indítványozta, hogy hamarabb szabadulhasson. A jogszabály szerint azonban az áldozatok biztonságérzetének növelése miatt a bírónak meg kell hallgatnia az áldozat véleményét is, mielőtt dönt a feltételes szabadságra helyezésről.

stories.title: Nem szorongást keltő, hanem támogató teljesítményértékelést akar a tanároknak az oktatási miniszter

stories.title: Óriási felháborodást váltott ki Franciaországban egy 11 éves lány meggyilkolása
stories.summary: A gyanúsítottat korábban már többször is megvádolták kiskorúak szexuális zaklatása és nemi erőszak miatt, de jogi lépések egyszer sem történtek. A kormány vizsgálatot indított.

stories.title: US House approves war powers resolution to rein in Trump's military actions
stories.summary: House lawmakers voted 215-208 on the resolution, with four Republicans joining Democrats, an outcome that saw cheers erupting in the House chamber in what is a clear rebuke of Trump’s war strategy.

stories.title: Albania: Fourth day of unrest as Trump-linked resort sparks protests
stories.summary: On the fourth consecutive day of protests, demonstrators clashed with police in central Tirana on 3 June over a luxury resort project linked to the Trump family.

stories.title: Video shows moment drone hits Kuwait airport terminal
stories.summary: Surveillance footage captured the moment a drone struck a passenger terminal at Kuwait International Airport, killing one person and injuring dozens in the latest escalation involving Iran.

stories.title: Greenland is part of Denmark 'for now,' US Secretary of State Marco Rubio says
stories.summary: The US is reported to have had 17 military facilities and more than 10,000 troops on Greenland at the height of the Cold War.

stories.title: Clash at protest against Kushner linked Albania resort project
stories.summary: Video shows security personnel arguing with demonstrators before pushing one protester to the ground and dragging him away. The incident took place during a rally organised by environmental groups opposing a luxury resort project linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of US President Donald Trump.

stories.title: Ukraine strikes St Petersburg oil refinery – video
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones hit energy and military sites in St Petersburg early on Wednesday, according to footage shared by Volodymyr Zelenskyy on X. Several long-range drones crashed into oil storage facilities as Russian air defences tried unsuccessfully to shoot them down. There were loud explosions and black smoke from the blazing oil terminal rose high above the city Ukraine strikes St Petersburg oil refinery Continue reading...

stories.title: Portugal: General strike causes disruption to services
stories.summary: Flights have been canceled and schools closed as unions stage a strike over government labor reform plans. It is the second major walkout in Portugal in six months.

stories.title: Bolivian ministers resign as weeks of protests against economic crisis rock government
stories.summary: The government has refused to rule out declaring a state of emergency and using the military to control the demonstrations.

stories.title: Israel's military campaign in Lebanon threatens to derail US-Iran peace talks
stories.summary: Israel continued to launch attacks at Lebanon, despite US President Donald Trump's heated phone call on Monday in an effort to salvage the peace deal with Iran.

stories.title: DRC Ebola outbreak could have begun as early as January, WHO chief says
stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus says the virus ‘had a big head start’ but that the response was catching up The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo could have begun as early as January, the head of the World Health Organization said, giving the virus “a big head start”. Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus also said the response was being hindered by blanket travel restrictions and highlighted high levels of community mistrust and low levels of contact tracing as key concerns. Continue re

stories.title: Japan sees shortage of plastic bags, trays and gloves, as Iran war-induced naphtha shortage worsens
stories.summary: The Middle East is Japan’s main source of crude oil, from which naphtha is extracted and used to make items including printing ink and plastics Takeaways, supermarkets, and bakeries in Japan are running out of plastic bags, trays and food service gloves amid widening shortages of the key plastic ingredient, naphtha, due to the Middle East crisis. The food sector accounts for nearly one-third of Japan’s annual plastic use of more than 8m tonnes, and price rises and shortages are hitting hard acro

stories.title: Rebel attacks in eastern DRC kill 30 people and hamper Ebola response
stories.summary: Islamic State-linked militia blamed for raids in North Kivu as governor says three patients with disease fled clinics Rebel attacks around a town that is one of the centres of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo have left more than 30 people dead over the past few days, complicating the response to the disease. At least 10 people were massacred in raids on three villages around the city of Beni, in North Kivu, in the early hours of Wednesday morning. Continue reading...

stories.title: A good life for the 99% isn’t a pipe dream: it can be done. Here’s how
stories.summary: Our plan is radical – but by transforming how we live on a finite planet, nearly everyone gains ‘An equal and habitable world is possible’: academics set out sweeping vision for planetary survival Imagine a future in which everyone enjoys high levels of wellbeing; where 90% of the world’s population doubles their income but works half the hours we work today. A world in which the bottom half of humanity sees its share of global wealth rise from just 2% today to 30%; a world where we consume enou

stories.title: Germany sees record number of discrimination claims in 2025
stories.summary: The federal commissioner for anti-discrimination has said the government's reform plans, the first in 20 years, are inadequate and won't provide the resources and funding needed to combat discrimination.

stories.title: Iran war: Even a peace deal won't fix energy crunch
stories.summary: The growing energy and supply chain crisis is unlikely to ease, even if the US and Iran step back from the brink. Experts warn that reopening the Strait of Hormuz and repairing infrastructure could take months or years.

stories.title: 2026 World Cup: 6 things you need to know
stories.summary: The 2026 World Cup is to be bigger than all of its predecessors. Whether it will be better is an entirely different question. DW fills you in on what you need to know.

stories.title: Kushner-backed Albania resort sparks protests, EU concerns
stories.summary: Donald Trump's son-in-law is linked to a megaproject on Albania's protected coast that has triggered protests and EU scrutiny over risks to flamingos and endangered habitats.

stories.title: Marjane Satrapi, author of 'Persepolis,' dies at 56
stories.summary: The Iranian-born author of the acclaimed graphic novel and film offered a deeply personal account of Iran's Islamic Revolution.

stories.title: Can Hawaii wean itself of oil imports?
stories.summary: Hawaii imports much of its fuel — and pays the price. From solar to geothermal, the state is searching for a way out of fossil fuel dependence.

stories.title: Germany's UN defeat: What went wrong?
stories.summary: Austria and Portugal have won seats on the UN Security Council, ending Germany's 40-year streak. DW examines the foreign policy questions raised by Berlin's defeat.

stories.title: Germans at Russia's SPIEF: Who's really there?
stories.summary: Russian officials and media claim German businesses are comming back to the St. Petersburg Economic Forum despite the war in Ukraine. But who is actually attending Russia's flagship event this year?

stories.title: FBI agents fatally shoot man holding hostages in California building, police say
stories.summary: Nearby buildings, including City Hall and the police headquarters, were evacuated and some roads were temporarily closed during the hostage situation.

stories.title: Kyrgyzstan elected to UN Security Council for first time in its history
stories.summary: Kyrgyzstan was elected to the UN Security Council as a non-permanent member for 2027-2028, marking a rare win for Central Asia.

stories.title: Grab what you can while you can: The new reality in the South China Sea
stories.summary: After years of watching China create land to back its expansive claims, others are doing the same.

stories.title: Robodogs and helicopters: One Mexican city's approach to World Cup security
stories.summary: In the shadow of cartel violence, BBC’s Will Grant got a first-hand look at security plans in Monterrey.

stories.title: Cuba’s fuel crisis leaves Havana’s streets overflowing with rubbish
stories.summary: Cuba is facing a mounting waste management crisis as fuel shortages continue to disrupt rubbish collection across Havana, the country's capital.

stories.title: France: Protesters rally outside CNews TV over alleged Kremlin influence
stories.summary: France witnessed renewed debate over foreign influence in the media on 3 June as dozens of protesters gathered outside CNews headquarters in Paris to denounce Ksenia Fedorova, the former head of RT France.

stories.title: Ayatollah says Iran delivered 'decisive blow' to 'malicious enemy'
stories.summary: In an unexpected statement on Thursday, Iran’s Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei said the “malicious enemy” has been defeated with “a decisive blow” to the US, in a possible signal of preparing the Iranian nation for an impending peace deal.

stories.title: Sherpa guide believed dead on Everest found alive after a week
stories.summary: A Sherpa guide missing on Mount Everest for nearly a week was found alive crawling toward base camp and reunited with his family, who had already begun funeral rituals believing he had died.

stories.title: Watch: Lebanon-Israel ceasefire deal made in 'hope rather than expectation'
stories.summary: BBC correspondent John Sudworth reports from the Beirut stronghold of Hezbollah support, Dahieh.

stories.title: US eyes stake in Azerbaijan's pipelines as economic ties enter new phase after Trump-brokered deal
stories.summary: Washington wants in on Azerbaijan's pipelines and energy infrastructure, a senior US official said in Baku, as the two countries deepen economic ties following Trump's White House peace deal with Armenia.

stories.title: 'Nothing left to chance' for Bayeux Tapestry's journey to London
stories.summary: The 950-year-old work will be as safe "as a baby" during its move to London, French officials say.

stories.title: There is no energy transition — only addition, experts say at Baku Energy Week
stories.summary: Grid infrastructure emerged as the forum's defining bottleneck on the last day of Baku Energy Week, as the IGU's chief of staff said that global consumption of oil, gas, coal and renewables is rising simultaneously, directly challenging the conventional narrative.

stories.title: Sri Lanka care home owner in custody after overnight fire kills 12, officials say
stories.summary: Police said initial witness accounts suggested that the fire originated in a storeroom where dozens of mattresses were stacked.

stories.title: Hezbollah official says group told Lebanon it rejects proposed truce with Israel
stories.summary: A top Iranian general reiterated Tehran's demand for a full ceasefire in Lebanon on Thursday and called for Israel to pull troops back to where they were when the wider war began.

stories.title: UK: Protest erupts over student murder
stories.summary: Protesters have clashed with police in Southampton over the December murder of finance student Henry Nowak. The fatally injured victim was handcuffed by police after his killer accused him of racist abuse.
